original_text: You prevent someone from committing suicide.
identified_property: lasting impact and effectiveness of the prevention

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: You momentarily talk someone out of a suicide attempt, but they remain in acute crisis with no support and attempt again shortly after.

variations.label: low
variations.text: You prevent someone from committing suicide, but the intervention is fleeting—they receive no follow-up care and remain at high risk of reattempting.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: You prevent someone from committing suicide and they receive some short-term crisis support, but their underlying struggles remain largely unaddressed and significant risk persists.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: You prevent someone from committing suicide.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: You prevent someone from committing suicide and connect them with effective ongoing mental health support, leading to a meaningful and sustained reduction in their suicidal ideation.

variations.label: high
variations.text: You prevent someone from committing suicide and they go on to achieve lasting recovery, rebuilding their life with deep psychological resilience and a renewed sense of purpose.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: You prevent someone from committing suicide and they make a complete, permanent transformation—fully recovering, thriving in every dimension of life, and becoming a source of hope and support for others in crisis.

quality_checks.reasoning: All tiers describe the same core act of preventing a suicide. The property varied is the durability and depth of the prevention's positive impact: from a near-futile momentary delay (tier 1) through ambiguous effectiveness (tier 4) to a life-transforming, permanent recovery that radiates outward (tier 7). Each step represents a qualitatively greater degree of lasting effectiveness.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because the prevention leads to the most lasting, deep, and complete positive outcome—total recovery, a thriving life, and a ripple effect of helping others—maximizing the effectiveness and enduring impact of the intervention.
